notes: Growth-stage B2B SaaS, workflow automation platform. Multi-product. Illustrative twin for tech vertical.

segments.name: Enterprise (>$10K ACV)
segments.description: Multi-year contracts with Fortune 2000 accounts; managed by direct sales

segments.name: SMB / self-serve
segments.description: Product-led growth funnel; low ACV, high logo count

competitors.name: ServiceNow
competitors.notes: Much larger incumbent; harder to displace at enterprise

competitors.name: Monday.com / Asana
competitors.notes: Stronger at SMB/mid-market with PLG motion

competitors.name: Custom in-house solutions
competitors.notes: Build-vs-buy is still a real loss vector at enterprise

prior_transformations.name: PLG motion launch
prior_transformations.description: Add product-led growth funnel for SMB on top of sales-led enterprise
prior_transformations.learnings: Logo count grew; SMB net margin still negative after 3 years

prior_transformations.name: Multi-product expansion (analytics, workflows, integrations)
prior_transformations.description: Move from single workflow product to bundled platform with 3+ products
prior_transformations.learnings: Multi-product attach rose to 40%; LTV up 35%

prior_transformations.name: AI copilot launch
prior_transformations.description: Native LLM-powered automation suggestions inside platform
prior_transformations.learnings: Usage promising; price increase met with customer resistance

strategic_priorities: AI feature monetization without alienating base
Defend enterprise vs ServiceNow displacement attempts
pain_points: SMB segment unit economics still negative
GenAI-native competitors raising at 50x ARR with no profitability discipline
Sales cycle elongating in enterprise (avg now 7 months)
Engineer comp inflation; large competitors poaching
Customer concentration risk - top 20 accounts = 35% of ARR
